title: ECG 783 Final Exam Review — Five Proof Problems: Wiener, Steepest Descent, Gradient Estimation, Leaky LMS & SER
summary: Five derivation-and-proof problems: (1) Wiener–Hopf equation W*=R⁻¹P, shifted surface J=J_min+(W−W*)ᵀR(W−W*), orthogonality E[e*Xₙ]=0; (2) steepest-descent principal-coordinate recursion V'_{k+1}=(I−2μΛ)V'_k, stability 0<μ<1/λ_max, learning curve; (3) central-difference gradient variance σ_J²/(2s²) and normalised perturbation P=s²λ_ave/J_min; (4) regularized LMS with reference vector W₀ — update, stability 0<μ<1/(λ_max+α), steady-state mean (R+αI)⁻¹(P+αW₀); (5) z-domain MSE proof, FIR reduction, SER C_n⁻¹ via matrix-inversion lemma.

title: ECG 783 Final Exam Review — Interference Canceller, Performance Surface, Gradient Estimation, LMS/Newton & IIR
summary: Five comprehensive derivation problems: (1) optimal interference canceller w*=σ_v²/(σ_v²+σ_q²), J_min, orthogonality; (2) 2-weight Wiener solution W*=[9/7,−1/7]^T, eigen-decomposition λ=3±√2, steepest-descent stability; (3) central-difference gradient variance kJ_min²/(2Ns²) vs. perturbation P=λs²/J_min trade-off; (4) LMS M≈0.19, τ_slow=25 vs. LMS/Newton M≈0.08, τ=12.5; (5) z-domain MSE, IIR sensitivity recursion, SER C_n^{-1} update, and stability triangle for 2nd-order IIR.

title: FedTrajPO++ — Federated Trajectory Policy Optimization for Tool-Calling LLM Agents
summary: ECG 618 Final Paper. Compliance-constrained federated trajectory DPO for tool-using LLM agents. FedTrajPO++ reduces violations by 94.6% and tool calls by 74.2% vs. FedAvg-SFT on ToolWorld-v2, via warm-started mixed SFT+DPO, modular skill bank, and support-aware aggregation. Not the raw-success winner — a safety-cost trade-off.

title: ECG 783 Midterm #2 — Problem 4: FFNN Two-Input Nonlinear System Modeling
summary: 6-input, 60-neuron FFNN (Levenberg-Marquardt, tansig/purelin) trained to model y(k)=cos(πx₁)cos(πx₂)+0.25x₁². Max approximation error = 0.000804 ≪ 0.15, R²=1.000000. Neighbourhood input design supplies local curvature context to the network.

title: ECG 783 Midterm #2 — Problem 3: Neural Network Character Recognition (E/C/U/S)
summary: Two-layer FFNN (20→4→2, tanh, backprop) trained in 6627 epochs (η=0.2, tolerance=10⁻⁴) to classify 5×4 binary characters E, C, U, S. Seven unknown test letters decoded as SUCCESS. Network tolerates 1-pixel deviations from training templates.

title: ECG 783 Midterm #1 — Wiener Solution, Leaky LMS & Nonlinear Adaptive Filter
summary: Three-problem midterm: (1) 2-weight Wiener solution for noisy periodic input, W*=[1,4]^T, convergence range 0<μ<1/3; (2) Leaky LMS derivation, stability bound μ<1/(α+λ_max), steady-state E[W]=(αI+R)^{-1}P; (3) Nonlinear tanh adaptive filter update W_{n+1}=W_n+2με(n)[1−y²(n)]X_n.

title: ECG 783 Midterm #2 — Problem 2: IIR LMS Adaptive Inverse System
summary: IIR LMS identification of the inverse of a 3rd-order IIR plant H_p(z). 5-weight adaptive IIR filter (a0,a1,a2,b1,b2) converges to w_opt=[1,−1.2,0.6,−1.3,−0.8] in ~3.4×10³ iterations (μ=0.001, N=80,000). Cascade H(z)H_p(z)≈1 verified via frequency response (0 dB, 0° phase).

title: ECG 783 Midterm #2 — Problem 1: Adaptive System ID via LMS, SER & RLS
summary: 10-weight adaptive FIR identification of a 3rd-order IIR plant using LMS (μ=0.0008, M=0.8%, T_MSE≈316 iter.), SER (α=0.995, T_MSE≈320 iter.), and RLS (λ=1, T_MSE≈1.65 iter.). Includes Wiener solution, weight trajectories, learning curves, and frequency-response comparisons.

title: ECG 783 HW2 — FIR Identification of an IIR System via Wiener Solution
summary: FIR modelling of the IIR system d(k)=0.5d(k-1)+32x(k)+32x(k-1) using the Wiener–Hopf solution. Compares L+1=2 (MSE≈787) and L+1=10 (MSE≈0.012), with impulse-response truncation analysis and frequency-response comparison.

title: ECG 783 HW1 — Wiener Solution for the Adaptive Linear Combiner
summary: Wiener–Hopf solution for a 2-tap and 10-tap FIR ALC on two signal pairs: sinusoidal input (Case 1) and square-wave input (Case 2). Includes correlation estimation, MSE analysis, and frequency-response interpretation.

title: ECG 783 Adaptive Signal Processing with Neural Network EXAM
summary: Four-problem exam covering 10-weight adaptive combiner (LMS/SER/RLS), IIR LMS inverse system, neural network character recognition (E/C/U/S), and FFNN two-input system modeling.

title: 数字生命的基石
summary: EEG 作为人类意图与状态接口，世界模型作为预测、规划与安全层，构建数字生命的基石模型。
